info.title: Atrium FP Pipeline API
info.description: Reusable Atrium Tower fire-protection CAD pipeline (ezdxf + matplotlib, with native AutoCAD DWG ingest via LibreDWG). Given a base architectural drawing — a native AutoCAD .dwg or a .dxf, provided by public URL or base64 — and a floor sheet ID, it produces an architecture-preserving NFPA fire-protection overlay (devices, routing, coverage hatch, legend, auto schedule, title block), runs a 3-gate self-verification (G1 architecture untouched, G2 device-in-room containment, G3 within drawing extents), and exports print-ready DXF + PDF + PNG plus a per-device traceability CSV and verification MD/JSON. Returns the verification verdict, the auto device schedule, the device total, and downloadable URLs for all artifacts.

post.summary: Generate an NFPA fire-protection overlay from a base architectural drawing
post.description: Runs the 6-stage fire-protection pipeline (base ingest -> FP overlay -> device placement + traceability -> auto schedule -> 3-gate self-verification -> NFPA print export). Provide base_file as a public URL (or base64) to a native AutoCAD .dwg or a .dxf, plus a floor_sheet_id such as A-111. TWO PLACEMENT MODES: omit `devices` for AUTOMATIC placement (one sprinkler per room tag, plus standard detectors/valves/call-points and routing); or pass an explicit `devices` array to place EXACTLY the devices you specify at your own coordinates (agent-designed layout) — typically after calling /inspect to read the rooms. For the highest fidelity, pass `zones` (rectangles/polygons): the tool lays an NFPA coverage grid of sprinklers per zone and draws routed branch + cross-main pipework with per-segment pipe sizes (Ø25->Ø150), and you can add the riser/ZCV/detectors via `devices`. Any combination works, and the 3-gate self-verification validates the result. Returns the verification verdict, the auto-aggregated device schedule, the total device count, the placement_mode, and URLs to the output artifacts.

base_file.description: Public URL to the base architectural drawing — a native AutoCAD .dwg or a .dxf. DWG is converted in-container via LibreDWG (dwg2dxf). Provide either base_file (URL) or base_file_base64.

base_file_base64.description: Base64-encoded bytes of the base drawing (.dwg or .dxf), as an alternative to base_file. Set base_file_name to indicate the extension (defaults to .dxf).

base_file_name.description: Filename hint for base_file_base64, e.g. 'A-111.dwg', used to pick the .dwg/.dxf ingest path. Default base.dxf.

floor_sheet_id.description: Floor / sheet identifier, e.g. A-111. Names the outputs and the title block.

nfpa_params.description: Optional NFPA 13/14/72/170 rule overrides. Defaults: nfpa13 = Light hazard, density 4.1 mm/min, max area 20.9 m2, max spacing 4.6 m; nfpa14 = standpipe Class II, landing valve DN65, hose reel 30m DN25; nfpa72 = smoke spacing 9.1 m, heat 57 C, mcp travel 61 m; nfpa170 = fire-safety symbol set.

output_formats.description: Subset of output formats to export.

title_block.description: Title-block field overrides: project_name, rfq, tenderer, revision.

devices.description: EXPLICIT agent-driven placement. When provided, the tool draws EXACTLY these devices at your coordinates instead of the automatic one-per-room-tag layout — so the agent designs the layout (e.g. fill each room on an NFPA spacing grid, clear of walls). Coordinates use the base drawing's units/coordinate system; call /inspect first to get room positions + extents. Sprinklers are auto-connected with cross-main + branch pipework. Omit this field for automatic placement.

type.description: Device type (aliases such as smoke_detector, manual_call_point, wet_riser are also accepted)

x.description: X coordinate in drawing units, within the drawing extents

y.description: Y coordinate in drawing units, within the drawing extents

tag.description: Optional device tag; auto-numbered per type (e.g. SPK-01) if omitted

model.description: Optional model/EMS label; sensible default per type

room.description: Optional room/zone this device serves (recorded in the traceability CSV)

zones.description: ZONED layout mode (highest fidelity). For each zone the tool lays an NFPA coverage grid of sprinklers and draws routed branch + cross-main pipework with per-segment pipe sizes labelled (Ø25 -> Ø150). Define each zone as a rectangle or polygon in the drawing's coordinate system (use /inspect for room positions + extents). Combine with `devices` to add the riser, ZCV, detectors, MCPs, etc. at exact points.

rect.description: Axis-aligned rectangle [x0, y0, x1, y1] in drawing units

polygon.description: Closed boundary [[x,y], ...] for non-rectangular rooms (use instead of rect)

spacing_mm.description: Sprinkler grid spacing in mm (default from NFPA hazard, ~4600 for Light)

200.description: Pipeline complete — verification verdict, device schedule, and artifact URLs.

placement_mode.description: 'auto' (room-tag algorithm), 'explicit' (the devices you supplied), or 'zoned' (NFPA coverage grid + sized routed pipework from your zones)

schedule.description: Device counts per FP layer, auto-aggregated from block attributes

overall.description: PASS or FAIL (PASS only if gates 1, 2 and 3 all pass)

gate1_arch_untouched.description: Base layer set + entity total unchanged

gate2_containment.description: Every device associates to a real room tag within tolerance

artifacts.description: Downloadable URLs for the generated artifacts

dxf.description: R2010 DXF: untouched base + FP overlay

400.description: Missing or invalid input (base_file or floor_sheet_id absent, or base_file could not be fetched).

get.description: Returns a single generated artifact (DXF / PDF / PNG / CSV / MD / JSON) for a given sheet. These URLs are returned by /run in the 'artifacts' object.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description: Floor sheet ID used in the run (e.g. A-111)

post.summary: Inspect a base drawing — return its extents and room tags
post.description: Ingests a base .dxf/.dwg (URL or base64) WITHOUT placing anything, and returns the drawing extents plus the room tags (name + x/y coordinates). Call this FIRST when you want agent-designed placement: read the rooms, reason about NFPA coverage, then build a `devices` list and call /run.

base_file.description: Public URL to the base .dxf or .dwg drawing

base_file_base64.description: Base64 bytes of the drawing (alternative to base_file)

200.description: Drawing geometry for designing a layout

extents.description: Drawing bounds { min: [x,y,z], max: [x,y,z] }; every device placement must fall inside these

room_source.description: Whether rooms came from the A-ANNO-ROOM layer or a fallback to all TEXT

rooms.description: Room tags with positions — use these to design device coordinates

get.description: Returns service status, the engine versions (ezdxf, matplotlib), and whether dwg2dxf is available for DWG ingest.
